rationale: Both abstracts define a specific, measurable gap and report paired area outcomes with clear comparators and P values. X is more polished for JICE style, uses standard scientific notation, and keeps the methods and results cleaner. Its conclusion is mostly calibrated to the within-patient design, although the statement that 1-year arrhythmia freedom was similarly high is only weakly supported without subgroup sizes or a formal comparison. Y is close, but the all-caps headings, 'plus or minus' wording, and slightly stronger clinical implication make it less publication-ready.
loser_weakness: Y most lacked editorial polish and certainty calibration around the 1-year arrhythmia freedom result.
